# Workshop Facilitator Guide — 10-Day Self-Build Programme

Last updated: 2026-05-29
For workshop leaders, build coordinators, and training organisers

---

## Overview

This guide covers how to run a Hestia Foundation 10-day self-build workshop — the format we use for the prototype build, future pilot builds, and training events.

**Workshop format:** 10 active build days, spread across 2–4 weeks
**Team size:** 4–6 participants + 1 facilitator + 1 documentarian
**Location:** Flat site, 200 m² minimum, with vehicle access
**Output:** One complete 37.5 m² weathertight Shell A + one trained team

---

## Pre-Workshop (4–6 weeks before)

### 1. Site Assessment
- [ ] Visit site in person
- [ ] Check flatness (max 5% slope)
- [ ] Check vehicle access for delivery truck
- [ ] Check water access (for mixing, cleaning, drinking)
- [ ] Check power access (for tools, or plan generator)
- [ ] Check mobile signal / internet (for documentation)
- [ ] Check weather forecast history for the season
- [ ] Identify nearby accommodation options
- [ ] Identify nearest hospital / emergency services
- [ ] Take "before" photos from multiple angles

### 2. Materials Procurement
- [ ] Order all materials 3 weeks before build (see `docs/build/PROTOTYPE_BUILD_SCHEDULE.md` for full BOM)
- [ ] Confirm delivery date 1 week before build
- [ ] Arrange on-site storage (under tarp, off the ground)
- [ ] Prepare materials inventory sheet (checklist on arrival)
- [ ] Budget 10% extra for waste and mistakes
- [ ] Have backup supplier contact for emergency orders

### 3. Tool Preparation
- [ ] Prepare tool list (see below)
- [ ] Check all tools are working (charge batteries, sharpen blades)
- [ ] Prepare tool kit for each sub-team (2–3 people per kit)
- [ ] Prepare first aid kit (comprehensive, not just band-aids)
- [ ] Prepare fire extinguisher
- [ ] Prepare generator if no grid power
- [ ] Prepare extension cords, power strips, work lights

### 4. Team Recruitment
- [ ] Advertise 6 weeks before build (see `docs/VOLUNTEER_RECRUITMENT.md`)
- [ ] Screen applicants: mix of skills, motivation, physical ability
- [ ] Hold 30-minute video call with each applicant
- [ ] Confirm attendance and dietary requirements
- [ ] Send pre-workshop info pack (safety, what to bring, schedule)
- [ ] Collect emergency contact information
- [ ] Confirm accommodation bookings

### 5. Permits and Insurance
- [ ] Confirm building permit (if required)
- [ ] Confirm landowner agreement is signed
- [ ] Arrange public liability insurance for the workshop
- [ ] Confirm volunteers have their own health/travel insurance
- [ ] Prepare liability waiver for volunteers to sign

---

## Workshop Schedule

### Day 0: Arrival + Safety Briefing (Evening)
- **18:00** — Participants arrive at accommodation
- **19:00** — Welcome dinner, introductions, icebreaker
- **20:00** — Safety briefing (2 hours, mandatory):
  - Site rules and emergency procedures
  - Tool safety (circular saw, drill, ladder, scaffold)
  - Lifting and manual handling
  - Working at height
  - First aid kit location and basic first aid
  - Fire safety
  - Heat/sun exposure (Algarve summer = 35°C+)
  - Hydration policy (water break every 45 minutes)
- **22:00** — Review build manual together (1 hour)
- **23:00** — Rest

### Day 1: Foundation + Floor
- **07:00** — Breakfast
- **08:00** — Arrive at site, tool distribution, roles assigned
- **08:30** — Site walkthrough, foundation marking
- **09:00** — Install ground screws or foundation pads
- **10:30** — Water break
- **11:00** — Lay pallet floor grid, level and shim
- **13:00** — Lunch (on site or nearby)
- **14:00** — Secure pallets to each other, install edge beams
- **15:30** — Water break
- **16:00** — Install OSB subfloor
- **18:00** — Clean up, tool check-in
- **19:00** — Dinner + debrief (15 min: what went well, what didn't, tomorrow's plan)
- **20:00** — Free time / documentation time
- **22:00** — Rest

### Day 2: Wall Panels (Prefabrication)
- **07:00** — Breakfast
- **08:00** — Arrive at site
- **08:15** — Briefing: today's goal is all wall panels prefabricated
- **08:30** — Cut I-joists to length for wall frames
- **10:00** — Water break
- **10:30** — Assemble wall frames (4 sections: 2 long, 2 short)
- **12:30** — Lunch
- **13:30** — Cut OSB sheathing to size
- **14:30** — Attach sheathing to frames, install insulation
- **15:30** — Water break
- **16:00** — Number and label panels, check fit
- **17:00** — Clean up, tool check-in
- **18:00** — Dinner + debrief
- **19:00** — "Panel assembly" documentation session (photos, notes)

### Day 3: Wall Erection
- **07:00** — Breakfast
- **08:00** — Arrive at site
- **08:15** — Briefing: wall erection day — biggest physical day
- **08:30** — Lift first wall panel (corner)
- **10:00** — Water break
- **10:30** — Continue lifting panels, bracing as you go
- **12:30** — Lunch
- **13:30** — All 4 walls standing, check plumb and level
- **14:30** — Secure panels to floor frame
- **15:30** — Water break
- **16:00** — Install temporary diagonal bracing
- **17:00** — Clean up, tool check-in
- **18:00** — Dinner + debrief
- **19:00** — Celebrate! Walls are up. Take team photo.

### Day 4: Roof Structure
- **07:00** — Breakfast
- **08:00** — Arrive at site
- **08:15** — Briefing: roof day — working at height
- **08:30** — Install roof beams (I-joists or timber beams)
- **10:00** — Water break
- **10:30** — Install roof pallet grid
- **12:30** — Lunch
- **13:30** — Install roof sheathing
- **15:00** — Water break
- **15:30** — Install roof membrane
- **17:00** — Install roofing material (corrugated metal, bitumen, or tiles)
- **18:00** — Clean up, tool check-in
- **19:00** — Dinner + debrief
- **20:00** — "Weathertight!" celebration

### Day 5: Windows + Doors + Sealing
- **07:00** — Breakfast
- **08:00** — Arrive at site
- **08:15** — Briefing: make it weathertight
- **08:30** — Cut window openings (if not pre-cut)
- **10:00** — Water break
- **10:30** — Install window frames and windows
- **12:30** — Lunch
- **13:30** — Install door frame and door
- **14:30** — Seal all joints with expanding foam or tape
- **15:30** — Water break
- **16:00** — Install external membrane and cladding (if time permits)
- **17:30** — Clean up, tool check-in
- **18:00** — Dinner + debrief
- **19:00** — "Building is weathertight" documentation

### Day 6: Electrics
- **07:00** — Breakfast
- **08:00** — Arrive at site
- **08:15** — Briefing: electrics — safety first, power off at all times when working
- **08:30** — Run cable from external connection point
- **10:00** — Water break
- **10:30** — Install breaker box / distribution board
- **12:30** — Lunch
- **13:30** — Run cable to socket locations, switches, lights
- **15:00** — Water break
- **15:30** — Install sockets, switches, and LED lights
- **17:00** — Test all circuits with multimeter
- **17:30** — Connect to power source (with experienced person only)
- **18:00** — Clean up, tool check-in
- **19:00** — Dinner + debrief
- **20:00** — "Power on!" moment — test all lights

### Day 7: Plumbing
- **07:00** — Breakfast
- **08:00** — Arrive at site
- **08:15** — Briefing: plumbing — water supply and drainage
- **08:30** — Install water tank or connect to mains
- **10:00** — Water break
- **10:30** — Run PEX pipe to sink and shower location
- **12:30** — Lunch
- **13:30** — Install drain lines
- **14:30** — Install sink and basic fixtures
- **15:30** — Water break
- **16:00** — Test water pressure and drainage
- **17:00** — Check for leaks, fix any issues
- **18:00** — Clean up, tool check-in
- **19:00** — Dinner + debrief

### Day 8: Insulation + Interior
- **07:00** — Breakfast
- **08:00** — Arrive at site
- **08:15** — Briefing: make it comfortable
- **08:30** — Fill gaps in wall insulation with loose mineral wool
- **10:00** — Water break
- **10:30** — Install internal wall lining
- **12:30** — Lunch
- **13:30** — Install ceiling insulation and lining
- **15:00** — Water break
- **15:30** — Install floor finish
- **17:00** — Seal all internal joints
- **18:00** — Clean up, tool check-in
- **19:00** — Dinner + debrief
- **20:00** — Interior tour — first impressions

### Day 9: Systems + Testing
- **07:00** — Breakfast
- **08:00** — Arrive at site
- **08:15** — Briefing: test everything
- **08:30** — Install solar panel / battery / inverter (if off-grid)
- **10:00** — Water break
- **10:30** — Test all electrics under load (kettle, lights, phone charging)
- **12:30** — Lunch
- **13:30** — Test water system for leaks under pressure
- **14:30** — Check for drafts, seal any gaps
- **15:30** — Water break
- **16:00** — Thermal camera check (if available) for cold bridges
- **17:00** — Structural check: measure any settlement or deflection
- **18:00** — Clean up, tool check-in
- **19:00** — Dinner + debrief
- **20:00** — "Systems operational" celebration

### Day 10: Handover + Documentation
- **07:00** — Breakfast
- **08:00** — Arrive at site
- **08:15** — Briefing: final day — document and celebrate
- **08:30** — Complete as-built documentation:
  - Photograph every wall, every joint, every system
  - Record actual material costs with receipts
  - Record all labour hours by person and task
  - Note any deviations from plan and why
  - Document "lessons learned" from the team
- **10:00** — Water break
- **10:30** — Install sensor network (temperature, humidity, energy, structural)
- **12:30** — Lunch
- **13:30** — Calibrate sensors, set up data logging
- **14:30** — Team debrief and feedback session (30 minutes):
  - What worked well?
  - What was confusing?
  - What would you change?
  - Would you recommend this workshop to others?
  - Would you build this for yourself?
- **15:30** — Water break
- **16:00** — Final team photo in front of completed Shell A
- **16:30** — "Handover" — symbolic key pass (even if no actual key)
- **17:00** — Clean up site completely (leave no trace)
- **18:00** — Final dinner — celebration, certificates, thank yous
- **20:00** — Farewells

---

## Facilitator Skills

**Required:**
- Construction experience (carpentry, general building, or similar)
- Ability to read and explain technical drawings
- First aid certification
- Patience and ability to teach beginners
- Fluent in the workshop language (English or Portuguese)

**Highly desirable:**
- Portuguese language (if workshop is in Portugal)
- Experience with workshop or volunteer coordination
- Social media / documentation skills
- Knowledge of electrics and plumbing
- Grant writing or fundraising experience

**The facilitator's role:**
- Not the boss — the guide. Everyone builds.
- Safety enforcer. No compromises.
- Problem solver. When something doesn't fit, figure it out with the team.
- Documenter. Capture the process for the manual.
- Morale keeper. Day 3 is hard. Day 6 is tiring. Keep energy up.
- Teacher. Explain why, not just how.

---

## Daily Rhythm

| Time | Activity | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|
| 07:00 | Breakfast | Hearty, high-energy food |
| 08:00 | Arrive at site | 15-minute setup time |
| 08:15 | Morning briefing | Today's goal, safety reminders, role assignments |
| 10:00 | Water break | 15 minutes. Hydration is non-negotiable in Algarve heat. |
| 12:30 | Lunch | 1 hour. Rest in shade. |
| 15:30 | Water break | 15 minutes. |
| 17:00–18:00 | Clean up | Tools checked in, site tidied. |
| 18:00 | Dinner | Debrief: 15 minutes max. |
| 19:00–20:00 | Free time / documentation | Optional. Some will sleep. |
| 22:00 | Rest | 8 hours sleep minimum. Tired people make mistakes. |

**Rules:**
- No alcohol during build days. Zero. Save it for Day 10.
- No one works alone. Always in pairs minimum.
- If someone is tired, they rest. No heroics.
- If it's too hot (>35°C), start at 06:00 and finish at 14:00.
- If it's raining heavily, stop and wait. Pallets get slippery.

---

## Safety Checklist

**Before every power tool use:**
- [ ] Check tool is in good condition (no frayed cords, sharp blades)
- [ ] Check area is clear of people and tripping hazards
- [ ] Wear safety glasses and hearing protection
- [ ] Secure workpiece with clamps (never hold with hand)
- [ ] Know where the off switch is
- [ ] Have a buddy nearby

**Working at height:**
- [ ] Use ladder or scaffold, never chairs or boxes
- [ ] Scaffold must have guardrails if >2m
- [ ] Tie off ladder at top if possible
- [ ] Never overreach — move the ladder
- [ ] One person on ladder at a time
- [ ] Spotter holds ladder base

**Lifting:**
- [ ] Lift with legs, not back
- [ ] Team lift for anything >25kg
- [ ] Clear path before lifting
- [ ] Communicate clearly: "Lift on 3... 1, 2, 3"

**Heat/sun:**
- [ ] Water break every 45 minutes (not optional)
- [ ] Sunscreen, hats, long sleeves
- [ ] Watch for signs of heat exhaustion: dizziness, nausea, confusion
- [ ] If someone shows signs: move to shade, water, rest, cool wet cloth on neck

**First aid:**
- [ ] First aid kit on site at all times
- [ ] At least one person with first aid certification
- [ ] Emergency phone number posted: 112 (EU emergency)
- [ ] Nearest hospital name and directions posted
- [ ] Defibrillator location known (if available nearby)

---

## Tool List

### Essential (must have)
- [ ] Circular saw (2 units) — with sharp blades and spare blades
- [ ] Cordless drill/driver (4 units) — with charged batteries and chargers
- [ ] Impact driver (2 units) — for heavy-duty fastening
- [ ] Hammer (4 units)
- [ ] Screwdrivers — flat and Phillips, various sizes (set of 4)
- [ ] Tape measures (4 units) — 5m and 8m
- [ ] Levels — 1m and 2m (2 units each)
- [ ] Squares — carpenter's square and combination square (2 units each)
- [ ] Clamps — 10+ quick-grip clamps, various sizes
- [ ] Ladders — 2× step ladders, 1× extension ladder
- [ ] Scaffold — if roof is >3m (rent if needed)
- [ ] Chalk line (2 units)
- [ ] Utility knives / box cutters (4 units)
- [ ] Pliers, adjustable wrenches (2 sets)
- [ ] Sawhorses (4 units)
- [ ] Wheelbarrow (1 unit)
- [ ] Shovel, rake, broom (1 each)
- [ ] Multimeter (1 unit) — for electrics testing
- [ ] Hose and water source (for cleaning, mixing, cooling)

### Useful (nice to have)
- [ ] Nail gun / brad nailer (with nails)
- [ ] Jigsaw (for curved cuts)
- [ ] Router (for edge finishing)
- [ ] Planer (for timber smoothing)
- [ ] Power sander (for interior finishing)
- [ ] Generator (if no grid power)
- [ ] Work lights (for early morning or evening work)
- [ ] Extension cords (4 units, heavy-duty)
- [ ] Tool belt (for each experienced builder)
- [ ] Tool box or storage bins (labelled)

---

## Post-Workshop

### Within 1 week:
- [ ] Complete as-built documentation (CAD updates, photo editing, cost reconciliation)
- [ ] Send thank-you emails to all participants with certificate
- [ ] Publish build update on website and social media
- [ ] Update GitHub repo with build photos and lessons learned
- [ ] Pay any outstanding invoices
- [ ] Return any rented equipment
- [ ] Store leftover materials for future builds

### Within 1 month:
- [ ] First sensor data review and publication
- [ ] Participant feedback survey and analysis
- [ ] Update build manual with workshop improvements
- [ ] Plan next workshop (if funding and demand exist)
- [ ] Apply for any relevant post-build grants or awards

### Ongoing:
- [ ] Monthly site visits to check structure and sensors
- [ ] Quarterly data publication on GitHub
- [ ] Annual structural inspection
- [ ] Maintain relationship with participants (alumni network)

---
